facts.text: The Presbyterian Church at Basking Ridge, founded in 1717, is one of the town’s oldest and most enduring institutions.
Basking Ridge was first settled in the 1720s by British Presbyterians fleeing religious persecution in England.
The Basking Ridge Presbyterian Church, a Greek Revival building completed in 1839 and expanded in 1869, earned a spot on the National Register of Historic Places in 1974.
The arrival of the Morris and Essex Railroad in the 19th century sparked residential and commercial growth along its corridor.
Basking Ridge boasts several properties listed on the National Register of Historic Places, reflecting its rich architectural heritage.
The Historical Society of the Somerset Hills preserves Basking Ridge’s archives and shares the town’s story through newsletters and exhibitions.
